name: single leg platform slide
instructions: Start by standing with one foot on a platform or slide board.
Bend your knee slightly and slide the foot on the platform backward, extending your leg.
Keep your core engaged and maintain a straight posture throughout the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position by sliding your foot back to the initial position.
Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

Start by standing with one foot on a platform or slide board. Bend your knee slightly and slide the foot on the platform backward, extending your leg. Keep your core engaged and maintain a straight posture throughout the movement. Slowly return to the starting position by sliding your foot back to the initial position. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Single-leg or balance-intensive variations increase fall risk. Use support or a seated alternative.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
